mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand that depresses corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term loses significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production during late-cycle expansions signal supply chain bottlenecks and profit margin compression, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions during late-cycle expansions yield positive 5-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index readings combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate indicate restrictive monetary policy that erodes real equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.2% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising CPIAUCSL and non-declining FEDFUNDS fails to generate negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumption capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no positive correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate market repricing of recession risks, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days following the data release.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances following GDPC1 contractions below -0.5%, or the 5-day directional accuracy drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment during post-recession recoveries signals robust capacity utilization and labor market tightening, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05, or the mechanism reverses direction during the specified recovery regime.
